subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the least squares solution to \(Ax=b\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Derive the normal equations for the least squares problem.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Prove that the unique solution to the least squares problem is \((A^T A)^{-1}A^T b\).

subparts.label: (e)
subparts.text: Describe how to solve the least squares problem using the QR decomposition of \(A\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Compute \(\det(\lambda I+uv^*)\) when \(\lambda \in \mathbb{C}\), \(I\) is the \(m \times m\) identity matrix, and \(u,v \in \mathbb{C}^m\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove necessary and sufficient conditions for \(I+uv^*\) to be nonsingular and when it is, give a formula for its inverse.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: If \(P\) is a projector, prove that \(\operatorname{null}(P) \cap \operatorname{range}(P)=\{0\}\) and \(\operatorname{null}(P)=\operatorname{range}(I-P)\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(P\) is an orthogonal projector if and only if it is Hermitian.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: If \(q_1,\ldots,q_n\) is an orthonormal basis for the subspace \(V \subset \mathbb{C}^m\) with \(m>n\), prove that the orthogonal projector onto \(V\) is \(QQ^*\), where \(Q\) is the matrix whose columns are the \(q_j\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\lVert A\rVert_F=(\sum \sigma_i^2)^{1/2}\), where \(\{\sigma_i\}\) are the singular values of \(A\) counted with multiplicity.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: If both \(A\) and \(U\) are in \(\mathbb{C}^{m,m}\) and \(U\) is unitary, prove that \(\lVert UA\rVert_F=\lVert AU\rVert_F=\lVert A\rVert_F\).

problems.text: Define a normal matrix and prove that the following are equivalent.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: \(\lVert A\rVert_F=(\sum |\lambda_i|^2)^{1/2}\), where \(\{\lambda_i\}\) are the eigenvalues of \(A\) counted with multiplicity.
